frameworks.name: rhythmic / oscillatory attentional sampling
frameworks.one_line: covert attention sweeps decision alternatives via an intrinsic ~11 Hz oscillation

frameworks.name: perceptual decision-making over multiple alternatives
frameworks.one_line: three-alternative choice as a testbed for sequential evidence sampling

frameworks.name: neural decoding of covert attention
frameworks.one_line: MEG decoding tracks the locus and strength of attention over time

core_question: What internal dynamics govern the covert sampling of information across multiple decision alternatives?
key_assumptions: decision-relevant information is acquired by sequentially attending to subsets
covert attentional state is readable from MEG via neural decoding
attention has a measurable locus and strength that vary across alternatives
manipulations: tracking attentional locus and strength across time within trials
key_findings: covert attentional strength fluctuates rhythmically around 11 Hz
shifts of attention between alternatives tend to occur at the trough of the oscillation
attentional shifts reset the ongoing oscillation
at the peak, attention sharpens focus on the currently sampled alternative
interpretations: intrinsic attentional oscillations form a core mechanism for flexible sampling of decision alternatives
oscillation phase organizes alternation (comparison) versus deepening (focus) of processing
